PhotoBoothSession.title: PhotoBoothSession
PhotoBoothSession.description: Represents a single user's interaction session with the Free-Time Machine photo booth, from photo capture and AI transformation to sharing portal generation and status tracking.

id.description: Unique identifier for the PhotoBoothSession entity.

creationTime.description: Timestamp indicating when the photo booth session was initiated.

originalPhotoStoragePath.description: The internal storage path or identifier for the user's original captured high-resolution photo in cloud storage. This path is used by the backend to retrieve the image for AI processing.

themeName.description: The name of the chosen thematic filter for the AI transformation (e.g., 'take a well-earned break', 'find my zen', 'learn a new skill').

refinementOption1Selection.description: The user's selection from the first pre-populated dropdown, influencing the AI transformation's style or content.

refinementOption2Selection.description: The user's selection from the second pre-populated dropdown, influencing the AI transformation's style or content.

refinementOption3Selection.description: The user's selection from the third pre-populated dropdown, influencing the AI transformation's style or content.

aiGeneratedImageStoragePath.description: The internal storage path or identifier for the AI-transformed high-resolution image in cloud storage. This path is used by the backend to serve the generated image via the sharing portal.

sharingPortalUrl.description: The unique, publicly accessible URL for the mobile-optimized sharing portal where the generated image can be viewed, downloaded, and shared on social media.

sessionStatus.description: The current processing status of the session, used for real-time tracking (e.g., 'captured', 'themeSelected', 'promptRefined', 'processing', 'completed', 'failed').

lastUpdateTime.description: Timestamp indicating the last update to the session status or any session data.

aiPromptText.description: The full AI prompt text constructed from user selections and theme, which was sent to the image generation model for this session.

errorMessage.description: Detailed message describing any error encountered during the image generation or session processing, if the session status is 'failed'.

qrCodeImageStoragePath.description: The internal storage path or identifier for the generated QR code image itself, which encodes the sharing portal URL. This is optional if the QR code image is generated on-the-fly.

definition.description: Stores individual photo booth sessions, each representing a user's interaction with the Free-Time Machine. Each session is owned by the user specified in the path. Includes a denormalized 'ownerId' field for robust authorization independence, set to the '{userId}' from the path. The 'sessionStatus' field explicitly tracks the real-time processing state.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the user who initiated and owns this photo booth session.

params.name: sessionId
params.description: The unique identifier for a specific photo booth session.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure for the 'Free-Time Machine' application is designed to prioritize security, scalability, and debuggability, strictly adhering to the core design principles and mandates. The central entity, `PhotoBoothSession`, is stored within a user-specific subcollection: `/users/{userId}/photoBoothSessions/{sessionId}`.

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):** This structure explicitly addresses Authorization Independence by leveraging a hierarchical path. Each `PhotoBoothSession` document will reside under a `/users/{userId}` path, where `{userId}` directly corresponds to `request.auth.uid` of the user who initiated the session. To further reinforce ownership and enable robust, self-contained security rules without relying on `get()` operations, each `PhotoBoothSession` document will include an explicit `ownerId` field. This `ownerId` field will be denormalized and set to the `userId` from the path. This allows security rules to simply check `resource.data.ownerId == request.auth.uid`, making authorization decisions atomic and independent of parent documents. The `sharingPortalUrl` will allow public access to the generated image, but the underlying session data will remain protected.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):** The `/users/{userId}/photoBoothSessions/{sessionId}` structure is ideal for Query Authorization Patterns. Users can only `list` their own sessions by querying the collection associated with their `request.auth.uid` (e.g., `/users/my_uid/photoBoothSessions`). This means the `list` operation is implicitly filtered by the path itself, avoiding the need for complex, inefficient, or insecure rule-based filtering. The rules will enforce that `request.auth.uid` must match the `{userId}` in the path for read/write access to individual sessions and their listing.

**Structural Segregation:** All documents within the `/users/{userId}/photoBoothSessions` collection are homogeneous in their security posture—they are private sessions belonging to the specified user. This simplifies rule writing by ensuring consistent access requirements across all documents in the collection.

**Access Modeling:** This design utilizes the 'Private Data: Path-Based ownership' and 'Use Hierarchical Paths for User-Owned Data' models, which are inherently secure and make ownership-based security rules straightforward.

**Data Clarity and Predictability:** The `sessionStatus` field in each `PhotoBoothSession` document explicitly models the finite state machine of the processing pipeline, providing clear and predictable status tracking without relying on derived states. Wildcards (`{userId}`, `{sessionId}`) are descriptive, and the `ownerId` field ensures radical consistency in authorization logic.
